programme.title: Cook's Country
programme.desc: Tasty tacos in Tucson, Ariz.; smoky mesquite-grilled tacos rasurados; portable burners.

programme.title: Sara's Weeknight Meals
programme.desc: An easy hack for three easy meals -- roasting root vegetables to make a puréed soup, an egg-topped hash and a Buddha bowl of grains and veggies.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: The worlds of landscape and aviation photography collide in a jet-fueled adventure in the wilds of California; the Sidewinder -- a low-level fighter jet training route.

programme.title: Riverdance 25th Anniversary Show
programme.desc: The reinvention of "Riverdance" immerses viewers into the energy and passion of Irish music and dance.

programme.title: Relish
programme.desc: Jewish-style potato latkes; kimchi jjigae.

programme.title: Christopher Kimball's Milk Street Television
programme.desc: The ancient origins of spaghetti and meatballs; charred red-sauce spaghetti and slow-cooked short rib ragu with pasta.

programme.title: Lidia's Kitchen
programme.desc: Gorgonzola celery; pumpkin risotto topped with gorgonzola; Venetian-style liver with a mushroom onion sauce.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: The renovation of the 1890s Victorian wraps up; touring the house, including its new back extension; the location of the next project is revealed.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Ask This Old House
programme.desc: Installing top-down bottom-up shades to block the outside view for a tiny guard dog; the anatomy of toilet tanks, common failures and how to fix a running toilet; landscaping a steep front yard.

rating.system: Dirección General de Radio, Televisión y Cinematografía

programme.title: Horizons From PBS News
programme.desc: Science, health, technology and environment experts and professionals address issues such as artificial intelligence, medicine and climate science.

programme.title: American Grown: My Job Depends on Ag
programme.desc: The future of the agriculture industry and stories of how it touches us all far beyond the table.

programme.title: Austin City Limits
programme.desc: Progressive R&B from rapper Lil Yachty; singer and violinist Sudan Archives.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The demise of the Shirewell patriarch sparks a vicious inheritance dispute when eldest son Lucian announces plans to break up an estate that's been in the family for centuries.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown must discover the culprit when a novelist is attacked at a crime-writing festival.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicia asks Father Brown to help prove a convicted serial killer didn't murder her beloved goddaughter.

programme.title: Austin City Limits

programme.title: Papa Ray's Vintage Vinyl Roadshow
programme.desc: Visiting Chicago's record stores including Dusty Groove, Shuga Records, Out of the Past Records and Beverly Records.

programme.title: Kris Kristofferson: Life & Songs
programme.desc: A 2016 concert celebrates Kris Kristofferson; performers include Dierks Bentley, Rosanne Cash, Eric Church, Emmylou Harris, Jamey Johnson, Alison Krauss, Lady A, Martina McBride, Reba McEntire, Willie Nelson and more.

programme.title: This Land Is Your Land (My Music Presents)
programme.desc: The Smothers Brothers and Judy Collins host a look at the evolution of modern American folk music; performers include Glenn Yarborough, the Highwaymen, the Kingston Trio and the Limeliters.

programme.title: Change Your Brain, Change Your Pain With Daniel Amen, MD
programme.desc: Dr. Daniel Amen maps the brain networks that drive physical and emotional pain and reveals his practical, brain-based strategies that help you feel better, think clearer and live more fully.

programme.title: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood
programme.desc: How pulleys work; a rainy story in the Neighborhood of Make-Believe.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: The Pea and the Princess; D.W. and Dr. Whosit
programme.desc: Prunella is directing the third-grade's production of "The Princess and the Pea," but nothing seems to go according to her plan; D.W. is determined to watch a new television show after her parents tell her she's too young to watch.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: Chris and Martin search for an obscure rain forest creature.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: The Hunter kids head to the state park to learn why leaves change color.

programme.title: Nature Cat: Nature Cat's Nature Movie Special Extraordinaire
programme.desc: Nature Cat and pals realize they've come to the end of their Nature Curiosity List; Sir Galahad gives himself the title of King, moves into a castle and steals pieces of nature to make it look more beautiful.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.sub-title: Daniel's Obstacle Course; Daniel Plays in a Gentle Way
programme.desc: Margaret gets upset when Daniel is too fast and too loud on the obstacle course; Daniel and his friends learn that sometimes it is important to play slowly and gently.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe and Jay use Miss Jean's field guide to identify their new butterfly friend; Phoebe gets courage to perform at the talent show.

programme.title: Inside California Education
programme.desc: A San Mateo school helps students focus by locking their cell phones away; the 2025 California Teacher of the Year; monuments in Orange County and the civil rights case that inspired them.

programme.title: Joseph Rosendo's Steppin' Out
programme.sub-title: Panama: The Heart of Nature and Heritage
programme.desc: Exploring Panama, from ziplining above a Panama City skyscraper to fishing off the coast of Punta Chame; meeting highly skilled local craftsmen; taking a wooden boat trip to dance with the Indigenous Embera in their village.

programme.title: Kitchen Curious With Vivian Howard
programme.desc: Chef Vivian Howard tackles the everyday questions home cooks face in the kitchen.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: Sunday is a day to celebrate food, dancing and culture; slow cooked pork sandwich; Vaqueria dance; a traditional Sunday family meal.

programme.title: NOVA
programme.desc: Four members of the Artemis II crew embark on a perilous 10-day journey to orbit the moon.

programme.title: Nature
programme.sub-title: Becoming Elephant: The Orphans of Reteti: Graduation Day
programme.desc: Following the journey of orphaned elephants at Reteti, the first community-owned elephant sanctuary in Africa.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Geordie asks Leonard for help when investigating the death of a professor at a computer laboratory; Will returns to Grantchester; Mrs C makes a shocking discovery.

programme.title: Grantchester on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Will is the only one able to reach a child from a farming family who is accused of murder.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Jo recommits to his family; Soames and Irene's Parisian honeymoon is cut short.

programme.title: Compass Points From PBS News
programme.desc: Experts analyze prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world.

programme.desc: Exploring America's national parks and scenic wonders around the world.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at Forsyte & Co. as Soames puts an end to Irene's Parisian dreams; Louisa faces a shock eviction.

programme.desc: Cora gets into trouble with Hooper; without the help of the police, Cora relies on Jimmi and Katy to unravel the clues.

programme.title: Murder in the Mountains
programme.desc: Hannes is propositioned by a former colleague. Nina and Lukas investigate the mysterious death of a young journalist.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Catherine is distressed when a newborn baby is taken from the maternity home.

programme.desc: Daniel Tiger and Donkey Hodie meet for the first time in Someplace Else, and Daniel's special visit takes the new pals on a surprising adventure.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.desc: Rosie helps Mom set up for story time when the Purple Knight visits the bookstore; Rosie and Javi make a strawberry horchata stand.

programme.desc: While helping Ms. Beaver construct a community garden the kids learn about rocks; Elinor tries to make her special dumplings all by herself.

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Holmes vs. Doyle
programme.desc: British historian Lucy Worsley delves into the complex love-hate relationship between Sherlock Holmes and the man who created him, Arthur Conan Doyle.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: After a pet detective turns up dead inside the kennel of a dog he was searching for, Barnaby and Winter are drawn into the world of dognapping, cat seduction, and animal investigation.

programme.title: Samantha Brown's Places to Love
programme.desc: Samantha Brown explores the emotional heart of travel and the people who change, challenge and strengthen a destination.

programme.title: BBC News The Context
programme.desc: A regular panel of guests from across the political spectrum discuss the biggest international stories of the day.

programme.title: BBC News America
programme.desc: Live news program produced out of BBC's Washington, D.C. bureau; host Sumi Somaskanda.

programme.title: PBS News Hour
programme.desc: Co-anchors Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett and correspondents offer in-depth analysis of current events.

programme.title: Wild Foods
programme.desc: Members of the Passamaquoddy tribe who have harvested blueberries for generations; exploring the dense forests, estuaries and harbors that produce Maine's oysters and lobsters; a chef creates innovative dishes with Maine seafood.

programme.title: Samantha Brown's Places to Love
programme.desc: Exploring Arizona's Route 66; the Petrified Forest; Jack Rabbit Trading Post; Winslow's historic La Posada; Kingman's Route 66 Museum.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Items reflect 250 years of American art, artifacts, crafts and collectibles; finds include a flag quilt circa 1880, a Tiffany Studios turtleback glass shade circa 1915 and an 1884 Edison light bulb.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Opposition in colonial North America to British policies over taxes, land and sovereignty escalates into violent confrontation; war gives 13 colonies a common cause.

programme.title: The American Revolution
programme.desc: Opposition in colonial North America to British policies over taxes, land and sovereignty escalates into violent confrontation; war gives 13 colonies a common cause.

programme.title: Amanpour and Company
programme.desc: Christiane Amanpour leads discussions about world issues and interviews with global leaders.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: NHK Newsline
programme.desc: This daily news program, produced by Japanese public broadcaster NHK, focuses on headlines and current events in Japan and other Asian countries. English-speaking presenters also cover other areas of interest, including business news, science and technology trends, sports highlights, and global weather forecasts.
